cases.next_action: Write one focused test through the public job-submission seam: submit a job, drive the deterministic scheduler, use wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout=..., description=...), then assert the observable status is "complete". Run it before production changes; make the smallest implementation change; rerun; refactor only while green.
cases.proof: RED must be an observed focused-command failure because completion is absent or wrong, not a timeout caused by setup. GREEN is the observed passing focused command. After each refactor, rerun it; before return, also run the nearest relevant group and record commands and results.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim TDD completion only after observed RED, focused GREEN, nearby GREEN, and recorded refactor/residual evidence; design or an unobserved expected failure is not completion.

cases.next_action: Write a focused test that observes DEBOUNCE_STARTED at fake time t0, advances to just before t0 + DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and proves no SEARCH_REQUESTED exists, then advances the remainder and proves exactly one SEARCH_REQUESTED whose timestamp is t0 + DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL. Run RED, implement minimally, then rerun.
cases.proof: Require an observed RED showing early, late, missing, or duplicate dispatch; then an observed focused GREEN establishing both the negative-before-boundary and exact-at-boundary assertions, followed by the nearest relevant group. Refactoring requires repeated focused GREEN.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim only the specified deterministic timing behavior; cannot claim timing correctness from eventual dispatch alone or without an observed RED and GREEN.

cases.next_action: Hand off to $diagnosing-bugs because the cause and trusted red-capable reproduction are uncertain.
cases.proof: Diagnosis must establish the exact symptom, cause, and a trusted reproduction that can fail for the expected behavior, or return a decision-needed packet.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not mutate production code or claim TDD/regression completion while diagnosis is uncertain; mutation begins only after the bug ownership gate is satisfied and implementation is authorized.

cases.next_action: Treat the setup error as an invalid RED. Repair the test environment or fixture, then rerun the focused test until it fails for the expected missing or wrong behavior.
cases.proof: Require an observed behavioral failure after setup succeeds; fixture, import, typo, and setup errors do not pass the RED gate.
cases.claim_boundary: Cannot claim RED or TDD progress from the event-loop fixture error.

cases.next_action: Record the focused failure as valid RED and the documented unrelated failure as a baseline/coverage obstruction. Implement minimally, prove the focused behavior GREEN, and rerun the nearest group while preserving both results in the return packet.
cases.proof: Require observed expected RED and focused GREEN. Also record the nearby command, the unchanged pre-existing failure, and residual risk or skipped-validation reason.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim the focused behavior passed after GREEN, but cannot claim the nearby group is green or claim complete TDD delivery while relevant validation still fails.

cases.next_action: Preserve the correct retry-count assertion and keep the repository-owned deterministic scheduler real behind the tested interface. Improve test setup or use the existing seam; if safe seam exposure requires support work, return that gap instead of weakening behavior or mocking owned code.
cases.proof: Require the original assertion to pass through the real owned scheduler. Any double must replace a genuine external boundary and satisfy the fidelity gate.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not weaken the oracle, mock the owned scheduler for convenience, or claim GREEN from a test that no longer proves the retry contract.

cases.next_action: Use a higher-fidelity substitute or integration test that reproduces the consumed timeout failure. If adapter contract tests exist, run them against both the fake and real or local implementation; otherwise explicitly record the unverified timeout-fidelity risk.
cases.proof: Require passing equivalent timeout-contract proof for the fake and real/local boundary, or a clear disclosure that only successful receipt is covered and the timeout path remains unverified.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim successful-receipt coverage only; cannot claim real queue timeout behavior is covered without fidelity proof.

cases.next_action: Refactor only behavior-preserving code within the tracer-bullet slice, one meaningful move at a time, rerunning the focused test after each move. Stop before the broader seam redesign and return it as residual follow-up evidence, recommending $codebase-design for the framed seam question.
cases.proof: Require focused GREEN after every move and nearest-relevant-group GREEN before leaving the tracer bullet.
cases.claim_boundary: May claim only the bounded cleanup proved green; do not widen scope, redesign the public contract, mutate a tracker, or claim the larger redesign completed.

cases.next_action: Return an incomplete decision and obtain the missing observed proof before any completion claim.
cases.proof: The minimum packet is Source Trace (behavior, source, seam, oracle); observed RED command/result and why it is the expected behavioral failure; observed GREEN command/result; relevant nearby validation or a skipped reason; refactor result or none; and residual risk or blocker.
cases.claim_boundary: Expected RED alone supports no completion claim. Completion requires every implemented behavior to cross observed RED before implementation, focused GREEN, GREEN through refactoring, relevant validation, and inclusion in the proof packet.
